report_title: STM32 고급 실무교육 — 코드 품질 감사 보고서
validation_criteria: Coding Standard Compliance (indent, naming, comments, no ASCII art)
Safety & Correctness (volatile, buffer checks, ISR safety, error handling)

ch01_ex01_led_blink.c.logging: ⚠️ No LOG macros (intentional: Ch02 not yet introduced, has TODO markers)
ch01_ex01_led_blink.c.notes: Proper HAL_GPIO usage, Korean comments throughout

ch01_ex02_led_pattern.c.logging: ⚠️ No LOG macros (intentional: TODO marker present)
ch01_ex02_led_pattern.c.notes: LED pattern array with configurable timing

ch01_ex03_button_polling.c.logging: ⚠️ No LOG macros (intentional: TODO marker present)

ch01_ex04_button_exti.c.logging: ⚠️ No LOG macros (intentional: TODO marker present)

ch01_ex05_button_exti_debounce.c.safety: ✅ volatile + timestamp debounce (no blocking in ISR)
ch01_ex05_button_exti_debounce.c.logging: ⚠️ No LOG macros (intentional: TODO marker present)

ch02_02_itm_port_direct.c.safety: ✅ ITM port ready check before write

ch02_04_log_macro.h.notes: CRITICAL FILE: All subsequent chapters depend on this pattern

ch03_01_spaghetti_demo.c.safety: ⚠️ SRAM overflow: g_lcd_framebuf[153600] exceeds 128KB (documented with comment)
ch03_01_spaghetti_demo.c.block_length: ⚠️ Some blocks >30 lines (educational: showing 'what not to do')
ch03_01_spaghetti_demo.c.notes: Intentional bad code for comparison. SRAM overflow is documented.

ch03_03_v02_layer_analysis.c.notes: v0.2 code analyzed with layer annotations

ch04_02_uart_interrupt_echo.c.safety: ✅ volatile on rx_data, HAL_UART_Receive_IT re-armed in callback

ch04_03_uart_command_parser.c.notes: Line-based command parser with buffer protection

ch04_04_uart_driver.h.notes: Driver layer interface — unchanged through Ch06

ch04_04_uart_driver.c.safety: ✅ HAL return value mapped to driver status codes

ch06_03_uart_dma_idle.c.safety: ✅ IDLE line detection for variable-length frames

ch06_04_uart_driver_v2.h.notes: Same interface as Ch04 — proves encapsulation

ch06_04_uart_driver_v2.c.notes: DMA+RingBuffer driver, same API as v1 (v0.6)

ch07_ex03_pwm_breathing.c.notes: LED breathing effect with PWM duty cycle sweep

ch07_tim_driver.c.safety: ✅ PSC 16-bit overflow check (83999 > 65535), UIF clear after UG

ch08_05_stopwatch_service.c.safety: ✅ FSM with enum states, no blocking in update
ch08_05_stopwatch_service.c.notes: Stopwatch FSM — reused pattern in Ch09/10/11

ch09_03_rtc_backup_register.c.safety: ✅ Backup register magic number for power-cycle detection
ch09_03_rtc_backup_register.c.notes: Backup register read/write with magic value check

ch09_04_rtc_alarm_setup.c.notes: Alarm A/B setup, #if 0 disables learning callback to avoid duplicate

ch09_06_rtc_driver.c.coding_standard: ⚠️ Contains header interface as comments inside .c file (unconventional)
ch09_06_rtc_driver.c.notes: RTC Driver (v0.9). Header interface embedded in comments.

ch09_07_alarm_service.c.notes: Alarm FSM — same pattern as stopwatch_service

ch10_stepper_driver.c.coding_standard: ⚠️ Some English-only log messages (e.g., 'Stepper driver initialized', 'Homing motor to 0 degrees')
ch10_stepper_driver.c.safety: ⚠️ Type mismatch: motor_position is int16_t but stepper_get_position() returns uint16_t
ch10_stepper_driver.c.logging: ✅ LOG_I/D/E (but English messages violate Korean comment standard)
ch10_stepper_driver.c.notes: Motor position type inconsistency may cause sign issues at boundary values

ch10_ex06_acceleration.c.safety: ⚠️ Calls stepper_step_forward()/stepper_step_backward() which are static in ch10_stepper_driver.c
ch10_ex06_acceleration.c.notes: Acceleration profile example. Static function visibility issue — would cause linker error if compiled separately.

ch10_clock_service.c.safety: ✅ FSM pattern, angle calculation from RTC time

ch11_i2c_driver.h.notes: I2C Driver interface, 7-bit address convention documented

ch11_i2c_driver.c.safety: ✅ Address left-shift (7→8 bit) inside driver, HAL return mapped
ch11_i2c_driver.c.notes: hi2c2 extern relies on main.h (acceptable HAL pattern)

ch11_ex02_sht31_raw_read.c.notes: Raw SHT31 read without driver abstraction

ch11_sht31_driver.h.safety: ✅ Named constants for all frame indices
ch11_sht31_driver.h.notes: SHT31 device driver interface with status codes

ch11_sht31_driver.c.safety: ✅ CRC verification on both temp and humidity, error returns
ch11_sht31_driver.c.notes: Including .c file is bad practice but functional. Should extract crc8 to separate .h/.c pair.

ch12_01_spi_dma_init.c.logging: ⚠️ Contains emoji in log: LOG_I("SPI1 DMA 초기화 완료 ✓") — violates no-emoji rule
ch12_01_spi_dma_init.c.notes: SPI+DMA init. Defines Delay_Ms() — duplicated in ch12_02.

ch12_02_ili9341_init.c.logging: ⚠️ Emoji in log: LOG_I("ILI9341 초기화 완료 ✓")
ch12_02_ili9341_init.c.block_length: ⚠️ Init command sequence block is long (~40 lines, but justified for 25-command init sequence)
ch12_02_ili9341_init.c.notes: ILI9341 25-command initialization. Duplicate Delay_Ms should be refactored to shared utility.

ch12_04_ili9341_shapes.c.safety: ✅ Bounds check on rectangle, Bresenham integer-only
ch12_04_ili9341_shapes.c.notes: Bresenham line, Midpoint circle, filled shapes

ch12_05_ili9341_text.c.coding_standard: ⚠️ Uses log_system.h, font data incomplete (ASCII 32-97 only, rest marked '나머지 생략')
ch12_05_ili9341_text.c.block_length: ⚠️ Font data array is very long (unavoidable for bitmap fonts)
ch12_05_ili9341_text.c.notes: 5x7 bitmap font rendering. Incomplete font table — only covers space through 'a'. Sufficient for demo but not production.

ch12_06_ili9341_driver.c.safety: ✅ SPI DMA transfer complete flag handling

details: ch08: stopwatch uses event flags, not blocking calls in callbacks ✅
ch09: alarm callback sets flag only ✅
ch11_sht31_driver.c: HAL_Delay(50) in sht31_read_temp_humid() is in main context, not ISR ✅
ch04_04: HAL_UART return → driver status mapping ✅
ch11_sht31_driver: CRC verification + I2C return checked ✅

uart_path.ch04: UART Polling → Interrupt → Command Parser → uart_driver v1 (HAL wrapper)
uart_path.ch06: UART DMA + Ring Buffer → uart_driver v2 (same interface, DMA backend)

timer_path.ch07: TIM2 Basic → PWM → Breathing → tim_driver (PSC overflow check)
timer_path.ch08: TIM1 Complementary PWM → Dead-time → Input Capture → One-Pulse → stopwatch_service FSM

rtc_path.ch09: RTC Init → Set Time → Backup Register → Alarm → Wake-up → rtc_driver → alarm_service FSM

motor_path.ch10: Half-step → Full-step → Wave drive → stepper_driver → Acceleration → clock_service FSM

sensor_path.ch11: I2C Scan → SHT31 Raw Read → CRC-8 → sht31_driver → sensor_service FSM

display_path.ch12: SPI DMA Init → ILI9341 Init → Pixel Write → Shapes (Bresenham/Midpoint) → Text → ili9341_driver

architecture_evolution.v1.0: Ch10 — Stepper + Clock Service (first full integration)
architecture_evolution.v1.2: Ch12 — SPI/ILI9341 LCD (complete smart watch ecosystem)

fsm_pattern_reuse.origin: Ch08 stopwatch_service.c — establishes enum states + switch-case + event-driven pattern
reuse: Ch10 clock_service.c — same FSM structure + RTC integration
Ch11 sensor_service.c — same FSM structure + timeout handling

critical.impact: Causes multiple definition errors if crc8.c is also compiled separately. Violates C compilation model.
critical.fix: Extract crc8 functions into ch11_crc8.h (declarations) and keep ch11_ex03_crc8.c as implementation. Include .h only.

critical.issue: motor_position declared as int16_t but stepper_get_position() returns uint16_t
critical.impact: Sign mismatch: negative position values (e.g., during homing) would be misinterpreted as large positive values by callers.
critical.fix: Unify to int16_t throughout, or use uint16_t with explicit wrapping logic.

critical.issue: Calls stepper_step_forward()/stepper_step_backward() which are static in ch10_stepper_driver.c
critical.impact: Would cause linker error if compiled as separate translation unit.
critical.fix: Either expose these functions in ch10_stepper_driver.h or use public API (stepper_move_steps).

major.impact: Multiple definition error if both files compiled together.
major.fix: Define Delay_Ms() once in a shared utility or use HAL_Delay() directly.

major.impact: Inconsistent with Ch04-Ch11 convention. Students may get confused about which header to use.
major.fix: Standardize to #include "log.h" across all chapters, or explain the transition in Ch12 text.

major.impact: Violates project no-emoji rule. May cause encoding issues on some SWO viewers.

major.issue: Font data incomplete — only ASCII 32-97, rest marked '나머지 생략'
major.impact: Cannot render uppercase letters beyond 'a', numbers work but limited. Educational context is acceptable but should be noted.
major.fix: Add comment clarifying this is a minimal demo set. Provide full font as downloadable resource.

minor.issue: Some English-only log messages (e.g., 'Stepper driver initialized')
minor.fix: Translate to Korean: LOG_I("스텝 모터 드라이버 초기화 완료")

minor.issue: Header interface embedded as comments inside .c file
minor.impact: Unconventional but functional. May confuse students about file organization.

minor.issue: No LOG macros (uses TODO markers instead)
minor.impact: Intentional (Ch02 not yet introduced). TODO markers are appropriate.
Exceeds 30-line recommendation, but justified for 25-command LCD init.
minor.fix: Acceptable exception. Could add section comments to visually break the sequence.

summary.score_rationale: Strong overall quality across 12 chapters. Consistent coding standards, excellent safety practices (volatile, buffer checks, ISR safety), and clear evolutionary progression. Deducted 1 star for Ch12 logging inconsistency (log_system.h vs log.h), 3 critical issues (C1-C3), and emoji in logs.

top_5_improvements.action: Fix ch11_sht31_driver.c #include of .c file → use proper .h/.c separation
top_5_improvements.impact: Eliminates compilation model violation, teaches students correct practice

top_5_improvements.action: Standardize Ch12 logging header to log.h (matching Ch04-Ch11) and remove emoji
top_5_improvements.impact: Consistent student experience across all chapters

top_5_improvements.action: Fix ch10_stepper_driver type mismatch (int16_t vs uint16_t) and static function visibility
top_5_improvements.impact: Prevents subtle sign bugs and linker errors

top_5_improvements.action: Eliminate duplicate Delay_Ms() in Ch12 files → shared utility or direct HAL_Delay()
top_5_improvements.impact: Prevents multiple definition errors in student projects

top_5_improvements.action: Translate Ch10 English-only log messages to Korean

strengths: Excellent volatile usage on all ISR-shared variables (Ch01, Ch04, Ch05, Ch06, Ch08)
Consistent FSM pattern from Ch08 through Ch11 (enum + switch-case + event-driven)
Clean architecture evolution (v0.1 → v1.2) with interface stability (uart_driver API unchanged Ch04→Ch06)
HAL return values consistently checked and mapped to driver status codes
Korean comments thorough and educational throughout (except minor Ch10 exceptions)
No blocking HAL calls in ISR contexts
Progressive complexity: each chapter builds on the previous without orphaned code
no_issues_found: Copy-paste fragmentation: No orphaned #defines or version comments ✅
Buffer overflows: All user-facing buffers have bounds checks ✅
